config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dari pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kebijakan yang merugikan orang kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan: kepentingan bersama dijaga, tetapi hak dasar setiap orang tetap dilindungi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach by government, employers, or even majorities. Individual rights are the guardrails that keep “the common good” from becoming a vague excuse for trampling minorities or unpopular people. That said, rights are not absolute in every case, since public safety and basic order sometimes justify limits, but those limits should be narrow and strongly justified. To me, the better principle is that society’s interests matter, but they should be pursued through a framework that starts with protecting individual liberty.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, menggusur yang lemah, atau mengabaikan prosedur yang adil, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas yang terlalu luas itu. Kepentingan bersama memang penting untuk ketertiban dan solidaritas, tetapi justru hak individu yang menjadi pagar supaya mayoritas atau negara tidak semena-mena terhadap minoritas. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan: kepentingan sosial boleh dibatasi, tetapi hak dasar harus tetap jadi titik awal, bukan korban pertama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests automatically outrank individual rights, because in the United States that logic has too often justified government overreach and majority pressure against unpopular people. The point about "public good" is real, but I do not trust it when it becomes a blank check to ignore due process, free speech, or equal treatment. In my view, society works best when individual rights are the default starting point and any restriction has to be narrow, specific, and clearly necessary. A healthy community is not one where the person disappears for the group; it is one where the group is strong enough to protect each person's rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan terhadap orang kecil. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi kalau diposisikan lebih tinggi secara otomatis, hak individu jadi terlalu rapuh dan mudah dikorbankan oleh mayoritas atau negara. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang membuat individu tunduk tanpa syarat, melainkan yang mampu menjaga ketertiban sambil tetap melindungi hak dasar setiap orang. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan “masyarakat di atas individu”, melainkan keseimbangan yang tegas dengan hak individu sebagai batas utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take automatic priority over the individual, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse government overreach and majority pressure. The argument for the common good is real, but once it becomes a blanket justification, it can easily trample due process, free speech, and minority rights. I also disagree with treating “society” as if it were a single moral voice, because in practice it usually means whoever has power deciding what counts as the public interest. To me, a fair society starts by protecting individual rights first, then limiting them only when there is a narrow, clearly necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
